titleStmt.title: Letter from William Loughnan to the Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 10 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Francis Loughnan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from William Loughnan (1881-1956) to the registrar at the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) thanking the college for bestowing a distinction upon him.
Colonel William Francis Loughnan was among the first contingent of the British Expeditionary Force to be dispatched to France after war broke out in August 1914. A serious injury received in November 1914 at Ypres forced Loughnan to stay out of action until 1917 when he returned to France and served for the remainder of the war.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: The King George Hospital, Stamford Street, London, England
Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
THE KING GEORGE HOSPITAL.
STAMFORD ST. LONDON, S.E. 10.III.'16 Dear Sir, I thank you, for your
kind letter, and ask you
to convey my thanks to the
President and Fellows. I was
very surprised and delighted
to find that I had been choosen
for this distinction, which I appreciate
more than I can say. Yours faithfully . W.F.M. Loughnan. The Registrar.
Royal College of Physicians.
Ireland. Macd.C
7.IV.16
